title: Why LLMs Need Retrieval
concept: LLMs are frozen after training; RAG connects them to external databases to retrieve updated, private documents before answering, eliminating hallucination.

exercises.prompt: What is the main limitation of standard LLMs after they finish training?

options.text: They automatically update their own code.
Their knowledge is frozen in time.
They can read minds to get new information.

exercises.explain: Once training is complete, an LLM's brain is frozen and cannot adapt to new information without being retrained or given external tools like retrieval.

cards.title: Frozen in Time!
cards.body: Once an AI model finishes training, its brain is frozen in time. It doesn't know about today's news, your private files, or what you had for breakfast. How do we make it smart enough to answer questions about things it has never seen? We give it a search engine!

cards.title: What is Retrieval (RAG)?
cards.body: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is a technique that connects a frozen LLM to external databases. Before the LLM answers, the system searches (retrieves) relevant, up-to-date documents and hands them to the LLM to use in its response.

cards.title: The Open-Book Exam
cards.body: Imagine an LLM is a student taking a closed-book history test. They can only answer using what they memorized before entering the classroom. Retrieval (RAG) is like giving the student an open-book test with an internet connection, allowing them to look up the exact facts right before writing their answer.

cards.title: Retrieval in Action
cards.body: If you ask a standard LLM about your company's secret product launch happening next week, it will fail because it wasn't trained on your private files. With Retrieval, the system searches your secure company folder, finds the launch plan, and hands it to the LLM to write a perfect summary.

cards.title: The Live Internet Myth
cards.body: People think LLMs automatically know everything on the live internet. In reality, they only know what was in their training data. Without Retrieval, asking an LLM about today's weather or your private documents will result in wild guesses (hallucinations).

cards.title: Retrieval vs. No Retrieval
cards.body: See how the system behaves when asked about today's news with and without Retrieval.

left.text: The LLM guesses the news or says it doesn't know because its training ended last year.

right.text: The system finds today's news article and feeds it to the LLM to write a perfect summary.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary benefit of using Retrieval (RAG) with an LLM?

options.text: It makes the LLM train much faster.
It stops the LLM from hallucinating by providing real source documents.
It deletes old training data from the LLM.
It translates the LLM's output into different programming languages.

exercises.explain: Retrieval systems gather relevant data from external sources and show it to the LLM, helping it generate factual answers instead of guessing or hallucinating.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Without retrieval, a standard LLM cannot know details about your private, unpublished notes.
exercises.explain: Standard LLMs are frozen in time. They cannot access your private documents or live data unless you connect them to a retrieval system.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the state of an LLM's knowledge after training.
exercises.sentence: Because training is complete, the LLM's internal knowledge base is completely _______.
exercises.explain: Once training is complete, an LLM's internal weights are frozen. It cannot learn new facts without retraining, which is why we use retrieval to supply fresh information.

exercises.prompt: Which AI assistant response shows that it successfully used a Retrieval system?

options.text: AI A: "I think you spent money on food because most humans eat daily."
AI B: "As a language model, I do not have access to your bank transactions."
AI C: "According to your fetched bank statement, you spent $42 at Target yesterday."

exercises.explain: AI C uses retrieval to lookup live external information (the user's bank transactions) to provide a factual answer. A and B are relying purely on frozen training data or imagination.

exercises.prompt: How does a Retrieval system actually help a frozen LLM answer questions about new or private data?

options.text: It retrains the LLM's brain on new data every single second.
It searches external sources first, then feeds the relevant info to the LLM.
It forces the LLM to guess randomly without reading any documents.
It compresses the LLM's neural network to make it run faster on phones.

exercises.explain: LLMs cannot dynamically surf the web or read your local files on their own after training. Retrieval acts as a bridge, finding the right documents and feeding them into the LLM's prompt so it can write an accurate, up-to-date answer.
